heroHeadline: Iran Seizes Ships in Hormuz; US Flash PMI Shows Stagflation; S&P 500 Pulls Back as FOMC Now 5 Days Away
heroSubtitle: Apr 23: Iran's IRGC seized two commercial vessels and fired on a third in the Strait of Hormuz as the US-Iran ceasefire held technically but strained severely — Trump ordered the Navy to 'shoot to kill' any boat laying mines. Brent crude approached $100/barrel; WTI at $93.31. US Flash PMI for April showed manufacturing at 54.0 (expansion) but input costs hit a 10-month high — the stagflation signal for the FOMC April 28-29 decision (now 5 days away). S&P 500 fell -0.26% to ~7,180 (Nasdaq -0.45%), pulling back from April 22's all-time high. Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation blocked by Sen. Tillis (R-NC) over DOJ-Powell investigation, adding institutional uncertainty ahead of Powell's May 15 term expiry. Moody's Analytics: 49% US recession probability; Goldman Sachs: 30%.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, S&P Global Flash PMI Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, Al Jazeera, NPR, 24/7 Wall St.
